div1.entryFree:
Graft, vb. 1) to insert as a scion, to make take root in and grow to: his plausive words he scattered not in ears, but --ed them, All's I, 2, 54. a servant --ed in my serious trust, Wint. I, 2, 246. the plants thou --est, R2 III, 4, 101. such rude society as thou art --ed to, H4A III, 2, 15. all the particulars of vice so --ed, Mcb. IV, 3, 51.
2) to impregnate with a scion: we have some old crabtrees that will not be --ed to your relish, Cor. II, 1, 206.
